qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Envoy gRPC ext_auth mode trusts spoofable x-forwarded-for metadata for client IP policy decisions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a forwarded-header trust bug on the Envoy gRPC Authorization/Check path, while the advisory is a separate case-insensitive encoded-slash path-matching flaw in Heimdall's route and rule evaluation logic.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's encoded-slash root cause. The only qualifying finding is an unrelated x-forwarded-for trust issue in the Envoy gRPC ext_auth request-context path.
evidence: The advisory describes authorization bypass caused by case-sensitive handling of URL-encoded slashes, where lowercase %2f is not rejected or normalized consistently when encoded slashes are disallowed.

The advisory patch updates Heimdall's route and rule path-processing logic to detect encoded slashes case-insensitively and to unescape path values without the old %2F-only handling, specifically in internal/rules/route_matcher.go and internal/rules/rule_impl.go.

The session's only final qualifying finding targets internal/handler/envoyextauth/grpcv3/request_context.go, where attacker-supplied x-forwarded-for gRPC metadata is trusted for Request.ClientIPAddresses on Authorization/Check; that is a different input channel, code path, and root-cause mechanism from the advisory's path-interpretation flaw.
